events.title: Sumerians build the first cities in Mesopotamia
Cuneiform writing is invented
Narmer unifies Upper and Lower Egypt
The Bronze Age begins in the Near East
Egypt's Old Kingdom begins
The Great Pyramid of Giza is completed
The Indus Valley Civilization flourishes at Mohenjo-daro
Sargon of Akkad founds the Akkadian Empire
The Xia Dynasty is traditionally founded in China
Babylon rises as a Mesopotamian power
Hammurabi becomes king of Babylon
The Code of Hammurabi is inscribed
The Shang Dynasty begins in China
Aryan peoples migrate into the Indian subcontinent
The Bronze Age Collapse begins in the eastern Mediterranean
The Zhou Dynasty replaces the Shang in China
The Phoenician alphabet spreads across the Mediterranean
Carthage is traditionally founded
The first recorded Olympic Games are held in Greece
Rome is traditionally founded
Siddhartha Gautama, the future Buddha, is born
Confucius is born in China
Cyrus the Great conquers Babylon and founds the Persian Empire
Qin Shi Huang unifies China under one empire
